local_results.title: Emaar Dubai Hills Estate - Sales Pavilion - Emaar

text_blocks.snippet: Emaar's owner is effectively its founder, Mohamed Ali Alabbar, an Emirati entrepreneur who established the real estate giant in Dubai in 1997, with major shares also held by the Dubai government via the Investment Corporation of Dubai. Alabbar serves as Chairman of Emaar Properties and is known globally for developing iconic projects like the Burj Khalifa and Dubai Mall.

list.title: Founder & Chairman
list.snippet: Mohamed Ali Alabbar founded Emaar and remains its Chairman.

list.title: Major Shareholder
list.snippet: The Dubai government, through the Investment Corporation of Dubai, is another significant shareholder.

list.title: Global Presence
list.snippet: Emaar is a leading global developer, with projects across the Middle East, Africa, and Eastern Europe.

list.title: Other Ventures
list.snippet: Alabbar also founded e-commerce platform Noon.com and real estate firm Eagle Hills.

With a proven track-record in delivery, Emaar has delivered over 63,000 residential units in Dubai and other global markets since 2002.

peopleAlsoAskedFor.question: Who is the richest real estate company in Dubai?

text_blocks.snippet: Emaar Properties is widely considered the richest and largest real estate company in Dubai, known for iconic projects like the Burj Khalifa and Dubai Mall, with massive market value, extensive project delivery, and global presence. Other major players include Nakheel (famous for Palm Jumeirah), DAMAC Properties (luxury developments), and Dubai Properties (subsidiary of Dubai Holding).
Key Players in Dubai's Real Estate Market:

list.title: Emaar Properties
list.snippet: The undisputed leader, a public joint-stock company listed on the Dubai Financial Market, known for large-scale master-planned communities and landmarks.

list.title: Nakheel
list.snippet: Famous for transforming waterfront living with projects like Palm Jumeirah and Deira Islands.

list.title: DAMAC Properties
list.snippet: A major developer focusing on branded luxury properties.

list.title: Dubai Properties (DP)
list.snippet: A key subsidiary of Dubai Holding, known for urban lifestyle communities.

list.title: Sobha Group
list.snippet: A significant developer with high sales value.

list.title: Iconic Projects
list.snippet: They built the Burj Khalifa and the Dubai Mall, key symbols of Dubai.

list.title: Scale & Delivery
list.snippet: Consistently ranks as the top developer by market cap and project delivery.

list.title: Integrated Approach
list.snippet: Develops residential, retail, hospitality, and entertainment sectors.

text_blocks.snippet: While Emaar leads in overall scale and recognized projects, other developers like DAMAC and Sobha are also major forces in the market, often ranking high in sales or specific luxury segments.
